title: rosa coriifolia subsp. subcollina
intro: Rosa coriifolia subsp. subcollina, also known as the mountain rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia subsp. subcollina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
